project_name: REFHYNE I — Shell Wesseling 10 MW PEM
status_detail: 10 MW PEM electrolyzer at Shell's Wesseling site (Rhineland Refinery). Commissioned July 2021. One of Europe's first multi-MW PEM electrolyzers in a refinery setting. EU FCH JU funded. ITM Power PEM stack. Served as the technology precursor and validation plant for the 100 MW REFHYNE II project.

data_management.narrative_summary: REFHYNE I is a 10 MW PEM electrolysis plant at Shell's Rheinland Refinery in Wesseling, Germany, commissioned in July 2021. It was one of Europe's first multi-MW PEM electrolyzers operating in an industrial refinery environment. Using an ITM Power PEM stack, the plant produces green hydrogen for refinery operations, partially replacing fossil-derived hydrogen. The project was co-funded by the EU Fuel Cells and Hydrogen Joint Undertaking (FCH JU). REFHYNE I provided critical operational data and degradation insights that directly informed the design and specifications of the 100 MW REFHYNE II project (GA-PR-008), including warranty negotiations and stack performance expectations.

power.grid_connection_detail: Grid-connected at Shell Rheinland Energy & Chemicals Park

sources.title: Shell commissions REFHYNE I 10 MW PEM electrolyzer at Rheinland — July 2021
sources.author: Shell Deutschland / ITM Power
